title: drawio-uml model (0.5.0)
description: Single JSON input for the drawio-uml suite, consumed by draw (model -> .drawio) and table (model -> .md). 0.3.0 redesign (backward-incompatible with 0.2.x): `layout` is a recursive cluster tree (row/column) that holds arrangement + boxes + node membership in ONE place; `nodes` are flat definitions referenced elsewhere by name; `views` are named node subsets. The 0.2.x keys node.cluster, options.clusters and options.layout.rows are removed. This file performs STRUCTURAL validation only; referential integrity (node-name uniqueness, every node placed exactly once in the layout, view/edge/cluster references resolve (an edge endpoint resolves to exactly one node, or one named+labelled cluster), cluster-name uniqueness) is enforced by draw/table at run time, NOT by this schema. 0.5.0 adds optional cluster description/remark (table-only, surfaced in a ## Clusters section) and lets an edge endpoint name a named+labelled cluster (a cluster-endpoint edge).

title.description: Document title (REQUIRED, non-blank). `table` emits it as the H1 first line so the .md is a standalone document (FR-T-11); under --view KEY the view's label is the H1 instead. `draw` ignores it (FR-D-08); a title-less model is schema-invalid yet still renders under draw.

nodes.description: Node DEFINITIONS (objects). Everywhere else (layout leaves, views) a node is referenced by its name (a string).

layout.description: Root of the recursive cluster tree (arrangement + boxes + membership). Omit for a flat diagram: dot then lays out every node and the flow follows options.direction. When present, it MUST place every node exactly once (run-time fail-fast). The root is normally an internal cluster (`clusters`); a leaf root (`nodes`) is allowed and means one flat group. A `label` on the root draws a box around the entire diagram.

views.description: Named node subsets, keyed by view name. `draw`/`table` --view KEY render the induced subgraph of that view: the master layout pruned to the selected nodes (empty clusters dropped, surviving boxes shrink, edges induced).

name.description: Unique node identifier; referenced by edges, layout leaves and views.

shape.description: One of: class, entity, object, component, package, box, usecase, actor, state, action, decision, initial, final, note. Unknown values render as box.

stereotype.description: UML <<...>> tag above the name. interface/abstract italicise the name; object underlines it.

fill.description: Box fill colour (e.g. #D5E8D4). Overrides the fill cascaded from the enclosing cluster.

stroke.description: Box border colour (e.g. #82B366). Overrides the stroke cascaded from the enclosing cluster's `color`.

style.description: Raw draw.io style string. Suppresses the shape preset and compartments.

description.description: Responsibility (single main line). Ignored by draw; consumed by table.

remark.description: Side note (origin, ADR, constraint). Ignored by draw; consumed by table.

source.description: Source endpoint: a node name OR a cluster name. A cluster endpoint MUST be a named AND labelled cluster (the edge anchors to its drawn box). Resolution is node-first, then cluster (run-time): a name that is both is ambiguous and fails fast; an unknown name fails fast; an unnamed or label-less cluster endpoint fails fast.

target.description: Target endpoint: a node name OR a cluster name (same rules as source). If equal to source, draw skips routing; an edge between a node and its own ancestor cluster, or a cluster and itself/its descendant, is likewise excluded from routing.

arrow.description: One of: generalization, realization, composition, aggregation, directed_association, dependency, transition, association. draw renders unknown/unset values as association; table outputs the literal value.

label.description: Relation label shown on the edge.

description.description: Ignored by draw; consumed by table.

remark.description: Ignored by draw; consumed by table.

cluster.description: One element of the layout tree. Arranges its contents along `direction`. Draws a dashed labelled box IFF it has a `label`; without a label it is an invisible arrangement-only container (used for unlabelled bands/rows). Holds EXACTLY ONE of `clusters` (child clusters -> internal node) or `nodes` (member node names -> leaf). A cluster may carry a `label` AND `clusters` (e.g. `consider`: a labelled box that itself contains sub-clusters).

direction.description: Lay contents out left-to-right (row) or top-to-bottom (column). Resolution order: this cluster's `direction`, else `options.direction`, else column. (Deliberately NO JSON-Schema `default` here, so a validator/IDE does not inject `column` and bypass `options.direction`.) For a leaf this also sets the dot rank flow (column=TB, row=LR), and drives invisible-edge stacking ONLY when the leaf's members have no edges among them (see `nodes`).

label.description: Box title. Presence => a dashed labelled box is drawn around this cluster. Absence => no box (arrangement only).

name.description: Short identifier for referencing this cluster from views (view.clusters) and table --cluster. Optional; needed only if referenced. Run-time rules: names are unique across all clusters (duplicate => fail-fast); an UNNAMED cluster cannot be targeted by a view or --cluster. PATH: a node's cluster path (used by table's cluster column and --cluster) = the chain of NAMED ancestor clusters from root to the node's leaf, skipping nameless levels, joined with '/'. A `name` MUST NOT contain '/' (run-time fail-fast). --cluster matches on '/'-segment boundaries (KEY matches KEY and KEY/*). A node whose deepest named ancestor is an INTERNAL cluster takes that cluster's path (e.g. the header node directly under `consider` has path `consider`, while a node under `consider`->`world` has path `consider/world`).

color.description: Box border colour (e.g. #9673A6). Named `color` (not `stroke`) because it does double duty: the box border AND the cascaded default `stroke` of descendant nodes. Cascade resolves to the NEAREST enclosing cluster that sets `color`; a node's own `stroke` overrides. The box itself is unfilled. LEGEND: clusters that are OUTERMOST labelled (a `label`-bearing cluster with no `label`-bearing ancestor) each contribute one legend swatch, deduplicated by `color`; an outermost labelled cluster with no `color` falls back to #888888.

fill.description: Cascaded default `fill` of descendant nodes. Resolves to the NEAREST enclosing cluster that sets `fill`, INDEPENDENTLY of `color` (a node may inherit fill and stroke from different ancestors); a node's own `fill` overrides. Does not fill the box itself.

description.description: What this grouping is / its purpose (single line). Ignored by draw; consumed by table (the `## Clusters` section).

remark.description: Side note about the grouping (origin, ADR, constraint). Ignored by draw; consumed by table (the `## Clusters` section).

clusters.description: Child clusters (objects) -> makes this an internal node. Order = arrangement order along `direction`. Nesting depth is unbounded by this schema. The readability guideline counts LABELLED levels on a root->leaf path (nameless arrangement wrappers -- the root, bands, rows -- do NOT count): recommended <=3 labelled levels (band / cluster / sub-cluster), run-time warning beyond 4. (The ARC example's deepest path is consider->world = 2 labelled levels.)

nodes.description: Member node NAMES (strings, referencing top-level nodes) -> makes this a leaf. Order is the stacking order along `direction` ONLY when the leaf has no edges among its members; with internal edges, dot's rank layout (per `direction`) governs placement and the listed order is only a hint.

view.description: A named node subset. The view's node set = `nodes` (explicit names) UNION the nodes under every cluster named in `clusters`. Edges are induced (only those with BOTH ends in the set). At least one of nodes/clusters is required; supplying both unions them. Rendering prunes the master layout to the surviving nodes: empty boxes are dropped, partially-selected boxes shrink, nested boxes are kept where members survive.

label.description: Display title (figure title / table heading). Defaults to the view's key (the map key) at run time.

clusters.description: Cluster NAMES (each MUST equal some cluster.name; unresolved => run-time error). May name an INTERNAL cluster (e.g. "consider"): its entire subtree (all descendant nodes) is included.

direction.description: Global default arrangement direction: used by the flat path (no layout) and as the default for any cluster that omits its own `direction`. column = top-to-bottom (dot TB).

column_width.description: Compartment column width hint (px) for class/entity/object boxes.
